title: What Parameters Are and Why They Matter

format.notes: No frontmatter. Sidecar present and valid. Exactly 6 sections in correct order.

length.notes: 1394 words within 1200–1600 target. 1394/230 = 6.06 min within 6–8 min target.

citations.notes: Primary [1] cited throughout. [2] and [3] cited. Bibliography entries match inline markers exactly.

no_forward_references.notes: Topics 3.1–3.4 referenced freely and appropriately. Fine-tuning deferred with 'covered in a later topic' (no forward number). Temperature handled as bare name + 3.6 pointer at line 132 only — not taught. No violations found.

sample.assertion: answer comes from billions of tiny numbers called parameters
parameter also called weight is a single number stored as decimal 0.347 or -1.82
weight matrix is a grid of weights that transforms one layer's output into the next layer's input
knowledge is distributed across millions of weights working together
compressed world knowledge — facts, grammar, reasoning patterns compressed into floating-point numbers
scaling laws: predictable relationship between parameter count, training data volume, and model quality
emergent capability: abilities appear suddenly as model crosses a size threshold
quantisation table fp16/int8/int4 with sizes and quality trade-offs
quantisation does not change the parameter count
more parameters cannot compress knowledge that was never in the training data

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to corpus. No new claims introduced.

beginner_readability.notes: All technical terms (parameter/weight, weight matrix, bias, scaling laws, emergent capability, quantisation, fp16/int8/int4) defined at first use with plain-language explanations. Concrete analogies precede abstractions throughout. No academic hedging language. Sentence lengths well under 25-word average.

formatting.notes: Paragraphs are short and well-chunked (no paragraph exceeds ~5 sentences). Sequences and procedures rendered as bullets and numbered lists. Inline examples present in Key Concepts (decimal value examples, GPT-3 vs GPT-2, rounding analogy for quantisation). Quantisation table effectively scans key trade-offs. Terms bolded at definition.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights appear in the reading text.

scope.notes: Fine-tuning mentioned twice as 'covered in a later topic' without detail — properly deferred. RLHF and RAG not mentioned. Backpropagation referenced back to topic 3.4, not re-taught. Temperature: bare name + 3.6 pointer only. Attention mechanism referenced back to 3.4 only. No scope creep detected.

summary: All 8 check areas pass. Format is clean (no frontmatter, sidecar valid, exactly 6 sections). Word count 1394 is within the 1200–1600 target; 6.06 min read within the 6–8 min target. All three resources cited; bibliography matches inline markers. No forward references beyond the permitted bare-name-plus-pointer for temperature (3.6). All 10 sampled assertions are faithful to corpus. Beginner readability is strong: terms defined on first use, concrete-before-abstract structure throughout, no academic hedging. Formatting is scannable with bullets, numbered steps, and an effective quantisation table. No assessment references. No scope creep; fine-tuning and temperature correctly deferred.
